title: Fix ESLint 9 configuration
body:
Acceptance criteria:
- `npm run lint` passes locally and in CI.
- Repo uses a valid `eslint.config.*` flat config.
- No deprecated lint bootstrap remains.

title: Make production build deterministic
body:
Acceptance criteria:
- `npm run build` succeeds in a clean environment without external font fetch dependency.
- Font strategy is local or otherwise reproducible in CI.
- Build steps are documented in README.

title: Add CI pipeline for baseline health
body:
Acceptance criteria:
- CI runs `install`, `lint`, `build`, and selected tests on every PR.
- Failing checks block merge.
- CI status is documented.

title: Add environment validation
body:
Acceptance criteria:
- Startup validates required env vars by environment.
- Missing critical vars fail fast with clear messages.
- Optional vars degrade gracefully and are documented.

title: Secure ChamaManager registration flow
body:
Acceptance criteria:
- Arbitrary callers cannot mutate circle registry or member registry.
- Only approved transaction paths can register circles/members.
- Contract tests cover unauthorized mutation attempts.

title: Protect /api/sign-as-payer from abuse
body:
Acceptance criteria:
- Endpoint requires authenticated or signed request context.
- Rate limiting and abuse controls are in place.
- Logs capture request volume, failures, and suspicious usage.

title: Protect /api/receipts from abuse
body:
Acceptance criteria:
- Only legitimate app users or signed clients can submit receipts.
- Invalid or spoofed requests are rejected.
- Failure and abuse events are logged.

title: Remove frontend as primary payout executor
body:
Acceptance criteria:
- Browser auto-execution is not the core production payout path.
- UI reflects actual execution model honestly.
- Fallback/manual execution, if kept, is clearly secondary and controlled.

title: Complete scheduler lifecycle wiring
body:
Acceptance criteria:
- Handler initialization, first scheduling, next-cycle scheduling, and completion are fully defined.
- The product can run a cycle without requiring builder intervention.
- The implementation path is documented.

title: Add scheduler recovery path
body:
Acceptance criteria:
- There is a defined recovery flow for missed or failed scheduling.
- Operators can detect and recover stuck circles.
- Tests or runbooks cover recovery.

title: Expand Cadence lifecycle test coverage
body:
Acceptance criteria:
- Tests cover create, join, contribute, penalize, execute, complete, and deposit return.
- Edge cases and invariant checks are covered.
- Test suite is runnable and documented.

title: Add adversarial contract tests
body:
Acceptance criteria:
- Tests cover unauthorized registry mutation, double join, double contribute, invalid execution timing, and bad recovery paths.
- Expected failures are explicit and documented.

title: Add real multi-user E2E flows
body:
Acceptance criteria:
- E2E covers create, invite/join, contribute, payout, and completion with multiple users.
- Tests validate behavior, not just page rendering.
- Critical happy path is stable in CI or staging.

title: Add operator observability
body:
Acceptance criteria:
- There is visibility into payer failures, receipt failures, scheduler state, and core transaction outcomes.
- Logs are structured and searchable.
- At least one dashboard or equivalent operational view exists.

title: Decide and unify receipt architecture
body:
Acceptance criteria:
- One canonical receipt strategy is chosen: off-chain only, anchored, or hybrid.
- Product claims match implementation.
- Unused/duplicative receipt paths are removed or clearly marked.

title: Improve transaction failure UX
body:
Acceptance criteria:
- Users see actionable errors for auth, gas sponsorship, receipt, and chain failures.
- Retry and recovery messaging is consistent.
- No silent critical failures remain in primary flows.

title: Add staging environment and release checklist
body:
Acceptance criteria:
- Staging is separate from local and production.
- Deploy procedure and rollback steps are documented.
- Release checklist exists and is used.

title: Add analytics instrumentation
body:
Acceptance criteria:
- Core funnel events are tracked.
- Dashboard shows onboarding, circle creation, join attempts, and contribution completion.
- Event naming is documented.

title: Improve onboarding for non-crypto users
body:
Acceptance criteria:
- New users can understand deposit, payout, and contribution obligations without blockchain knowledge.
- Onboarding copy is plain-language and trustworthy.
- Drop-off points are reduced in user testing.

title: Add invite/share flow
body:
Acceptance criteria:
- Circle creators can invite others with a clean share flow.
- Join path is simple on mobile and desktop.
- Invite acceptance is trackable.

title: Improve trust and reputation UX
body:
Acceptance criteria:
- Users can understand what reputation means and how it is earned.
- Reputation helps decision-making without over-claiming.
- UI explains risk clearly.

title: Build lightweight operator/admin console
body:
Acceptance criteria:
- Operators can inspect circle status, recent failures, and support-critical states.
- No direct dangerous actions are exposed without controls.
- Console improves incident response time.

title: Mobile usability pass
body:
Acceptance criteria:
- Core flows are smooth on mobile.
- Forms, nav, and transaction feedback are readable and usable on small screens.
- No major overflow or interaction issues remain.

title: Pilot program setup
body:
Acceptance criteria:
- Pilot cohort, user rules, value limits, and success criteria are defined.
- Feedback collection process is in place.
- Support ownership is defined.

title: Pricing and sponsorship strategy
body:
Acceptance criteria:
- There is a written decision on sponsored gas policy.
- Early pricing hypothesis exists, even if not user-facing yet.
- Cost envelope is modeled for pilot usage.

title: Mainnet readiness review
body:
Acceptance criteria:
- Mainnet blockers are listed explicitly.
- Contract addresses, infra assumptions, and operational controls are reviewed.
- Output is a go/no-go recommendation, not just optimism.

title: Market-fit decision memo
body:
Acceptance criteria:
- Pilot results are summarized with metrics and qualitative findings.
- Recommendation is explicit: continue, pivot, narrow segment, or reposition as infra.
- Next-quarter priorities follow from the memo.
